invalid_review_attempts.reason_invalid: Result was not JSON-only and began with prose/tool-call-shaped text; not counted as valid review evidence.

review_questions: Does CP00-146 cover exactly the 10 planned Risk A units in order?
Can any CP00-146 row prove security trimming, emit audit event expectations, write permission fixtures, or execute allowed/denied/cross-tenant/leak-prevention tests?
Can any synthetic fixture row evaluate permission matrix, view, or search decisions or load hidden fixture payloads?
Are unauthorized counts, unauthorized object names, hidden security trim predicates, and audit payloads suppressed?
Does CP00-146 hand off cleanly from CP00-145 to CP00-147 without implementing LDIP or splitting HRX?

findings.title: Core catalog implementation not present in review diff
findings.details: The new catalog module backing all eleven CP00-146 exports (createAuditComplianceCp146CoveredUnitIds, ...Catalog, ...Boundary, ...Manifest, Hermes/Claude packets, CloseoutHandoff, run...Case, validate...Coverage, plus the two constants) is an untracked file (git status shows '??') and was not included in the provided diff. Its descriptor-generation logic and the actual enforcement of the no-write / no-evaluate / no-leak / hidden-field-suppression guarantees could not be directly inspected. Correctness is inferred only from node --check passing, the 58 passing tests including the CP00-146 suite, and the passing RP03 validator, all of which assert the values the module reports about itself. Everything that WAS in the diff is internally consistent: 10 ordered units RP03.P06.M05.S16-RP03.P06.M06.S03 (7 M05 + 3 M06), deliverable counts 4/4/2 and evidence modes 7/3 both summing to 10, schema bump v0.12->v0.13 mirrored in the validator, 11 contract exports all consumed by test/validator, 51 no_write_attestation fields all validated false, handoff CP00-145 -> CP00-146 -> CP00-147 at RP03.P06.M06.S04, model claude-opus-4-8 read_only, and implements_ldip/splits_hrx_product false.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the catalog source is committed alongside the contract/test/validator changes and that an independent read of it verifies hidden-field suppression and the absence of any real permission/audit/fixture/test execution paths, since the test and validator only assert the booleans the module emits about itself rather than independently proving no side effects.

required_adjudication: Verify packages/audit/src/permission-matrix-security-fixture-boundary-catalog.js is committed for the CP00-146 closeout and read it directly to confirm its descriptor-only, no-write, no-evaluate (permission matrix/view/search), and hidden-source-field suppression behavior; the review diff did not include this file, so those guarantees are currently inferred solely from the passing tests and RP03 validator.
